description_long: This is a wooden picket fence section featuring two robust square posts with decorative caps at each end. The fence has a gently arched top edge formed by evenly spaced vertical pickets, each with a rounded top. Two horizontal rails connect the pickets and provide structural support, with the lower rail set closer to the ground. The wood grain is visible throughout, giving the fence a natural, unfinished appearance. The posts are thicker and more ornate than the pickets, with subtle beveling and a pronounced cap. This type of fence is commonly used in residential yards, gardens, or parks to demarcate boundaries while maintaining an inviting aesthetic.
description: A wooden picket fence section with an arched top, decorative post caps, and visible wood grain. It features two sturdy posts, rounded pickets, and two horizontal rails.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness and profile of a single fence post with its decorative cap.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full fence section, arched picket tops, two posts with caps, and horizontal rails.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, highlighting the post's profile and cap.
description_view_3: Rear view similar to the front, showing the picket arrangement, rails, and post details from the back.
